records.ety: [OE. benethe, bineoðen, AS. beneoðan, benyðan; pref. be- + neoðan, nyðan, downward, beneath, akin to E. nether. See Nether.]

defs: Lower in place, with something directly over or on; under; underneath; hence, at the foot of.
quotes: Beneath a rude and nameless stone he lies.

defs: Under, in relation to something that is superior, or that oppresses or burdens.
quotes: Our country sinks beneath the yoke.

defs: Lower in rank, dignity, or excellence than; as, brutes are beneath man; man is beneath angels in the scale of beings. Hence: Unworthy of; unbecoming.
quotes: He will do nothing that is beneath his high station.
The earth you take from beneath will be barren.

defs: Below, as opposed to heaven, or to any superior region or position; as, in earth beneath.
